verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचइत्थं पृथुमभिष्टूय रुषा प्रस्फुरिताधरम् ।पुनराहावनिर्भीता संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaitthaṁ pṛthum abhiṣṭūyaruṣā prasphuritādharampunar āhāvanir bhītāsaṁstabhyātmānam ātmanā
verses.text: The great saint Maitreya continued to address Vidura: My dear Vidura, at that time, after the planet earth finished her prayers, King Pṛthu was still not pacified, and his lips trembled in great anger. Although the planet earth was frightened, she made up her mind and began to speak as follows in order to convince the King.

verses.sanskrit: सन्नियच्छाभिभो मन्युं निबोध श्रावितं च मे ।सर्वत: सारमादत्ते यथा मधुकरो बुध: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: sanniyacchābhibho manyuṁnibodha śrāvitaṁ ca mesarvataḥ sāram ādatteyathā madhu-karo budhaḥ
verses.text: My dear Lord, please pacify your anger completely and hear patiently whatever I submit before you. Please turn your kind attention to this. I may be very poor, but a learned man takes the essence of knowledge from all places, just as a bumblebee collects honey from each and every flower.

verses.sanskrit: अस्मिँल्लोकेऽथवामुष्मिन्मुनिभिस्तत्त्वदर्शिभि: ।द‍ृष्टा योगा: प्रयुक्ताश्च पुंसां श्रेय:प्रसिद्धये ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: asmil loke ’thavāmuṣminmunibhis tattva-darśibhiḥdṛṣṭā yogāḥ prayuktāś capuṁsāṁ śreyaḥ-prasiddhaye
verses.text: To benefit all human society, not only in this life but in the next, the great seers and sages have prescribed various methods conducive to the prosperity of the people in general.

verses.sanskrit: तानातिष्ठति य: सम्यगुपायान् पूर्वदर्शितान् ।अवर: श्रद्धयोपेत उपेयान् विन्दतेऽञ्जसा ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān ātiṣṭhati yaḥ samyagupāyān pūrva-darśitānavaraḥ śraddhayopetaupeyān vindate ’ñjasā
verses.text: One who follows the principles and instructions enjoined by the great sages of the past can utilize these instructions for practical purposes. Such a person can very easily enjoy life and pleasures.

verses.sanskrit: ताननाद‍ृत्य योऽविद्वानर्थानारभते स्वयम् ।तस्य व्यभिचरन्त्यर्था आरब्धाश्च पुन: पुन: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān anādṛtya yo ’vidvānarthān ārabhate svayamtasya vyabhicaranty arthāārabdhāś ca punaḥ punaḥ
verses.text: A foolish person who manufactures his own ways and means through mental speculation and does not recognize the authority of the sages who lay down unimpeachable directions is simply unsuccessful again and again in his attempts.

verses.sanskrit: पुरा सृष्टा ह्योषधयो ब्रह्मणा या विशाम्पते ।भुज्यमाना मया द‍ृष्टा असद्‌भिरधृतव्रतै: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: purā sṛṣṭā hy oṣadhayobrahmaṇā yā viśāmpatebhujyamānā mayā dṛṣṭāasadbhir adhṛta-vrataiḥ
verses.text: My dear King, the seeds, roots, herbs and grains, which were created by Lord Brahmā in the past, are now being used by nondevotees, who are devoid of all spiritual understanding.

verses.sanskrit: अपालितानाद‍ृता च भवद्‌भिर्लोकपालकै: ।चोरीभूतेऽथ लोकेऽहं यज्ञार्थेऽग्रसमोषधी: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: apālitānādṛtā cabhavadbhir loka-pālakaiḥcorī-bhūte ’tha loke ’haṁyajñārthe ’grasam oṣadhīḥ
verses.text: My dear King, not only are grains and herbs being used by nondevotees, but, as far as I am concerned, I am not being properly maintained. Indeed, I am being neglected by kings who are not punishing these rascals who have turned into thieves by using grains for sense gratification. Consequently I have hidden all these seeds, which were meant for the performance of sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: नूनं ता वीरुध: क्षीणा मयि कालेन भूयसा ।तत्र योगेन द‍ृष्टेन भवानादातुमर्हति ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: nūnaṁ tā vīrudhaḥ kṣīṇāmayi kālena bhūyasātatra yogena dṛṣṭenabhavān ādātum arhati
verses.text: Due to being stocked for a very long time, all the grain seeds within me have certainly deteriorated. Therefore you should immediately arrange to take these seeds out by the standard process, which is recommended by the ācāryas or śāstras.

verses.sanskrit: वत्सं कल्पय मे वीर येनाहं वत्सला तव ।धोक्ष्ये क्षीरमयान्कामाननुरूपं च दोहनम् ॥ ९ ॥दोग्धारं च महाबाहो भूतानां भूतभावन ।अन्नमीप्सितमूर्जस्वद्भगवान् वाञ्छते यदि ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: vatsaṁ kalpaya me vīrayenāhaṁ vatsalā tavadhokṣye kṣīramayān kāmānanurūpaṁ ca dohanam
verses.text: O great hero, protector of living entities, if you desire to relieve the living entities by supplying them sufficient grain, and if you desire to nourish them by taking milk from me, you should make arrangements to bring a calf suitable for this purpose and a pot in which the milk can be kept, as well as a milkman to do the work. Since I will be very much affectionate towards my calf, your desire to take milk from me will be fulfilled.

verses.sanskrit: समां च कुरु मां राजन्देववृष्टं यथा पय: ।अपर्तावपि भद्रं ते उपावर्तेत मे विभो ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: samāṁ ca kuru māṁ rājandeva-vṛṣṭaṁ yathā payaḥapartāv api bhadraṁ teupāvarteta me vibho
verses.text: My dear King, may I inform you that you have to make the entire surface of the globe level. This will help me, even when the rainy season has ceased. Rainfall comes by the mercy of King Indra. Rainfall will remain on the surface of the globe, always keeping the earth moistened, and thus it will be auspicious for all kinds of production.

verses.sanskrit: इति प्रियं हितं वाक्यं भुव आदाय भूपति: ।वत्सं कृत्वा मनुं पाणावदुहत्सकलौषधी: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti priyaṁ hitaṁ vākyaṁbhuva ādāya bhūpatiḥvatsaṁ kṛtvā manuṁ pāṇāvaduhat sakalauṣadhīḥ
verses.text: After hearing the auspicious and pleasing words of the planet earth, the King accepted them. He then transformed Svāyambhuva Manu into a calf and milked all the herbs and grains from the earth in the form of a cow, keeping them in his cupped hands.

verses.sanskrit: तथापरे च सर्वत्र सारमाददते बुधा: ।ततोऽन्ये च यथाकामं दुदुहु: पृथुभाविताम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāpare ca sarvatrasāram ādadate budhāḥtato ’nye ca yathā-kāmaṁduduhuḥ pṛthu-bhāvitām
verses.text: Others, who were as intelligent as King Pṛthu, also took the essence out of the earthly planet. Indeed, everyone took this opportunity to follow in the footsteps of King Pṛthu and get whatever he desired from the planet earth.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषयो दुदुहुर्देवीमिन्द्रियेष्वथ सत्तम ।वत्सं बृहस्पतिं कृत्वा पयश्छन्दोमयं शुचि ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ṛṣayo duduhur devīmindriyeṣv atha sattamavatsaṁ bṛhaspatiṁ kṛtvāpayaś chandomayaṁ śuci
verses.text: All the great sages transformed Bṛhaspati into a calf, and making the senses into a pot, they milked all kinds of Vedic knowledge to purify words, mind and hearing.

verses.sanskrit: कृत्वा वत्सं सुरगणा इन्द्रं सोममदूदुहन् ।हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण वीर्यमोजो बलं पय: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛtvā vatsaṁ sura-gaṇāindraṁ somam adūduhanhiraṇmayena pātreṇavīryam ojo balaṁ payaḥ
verses.text: All the demigods made Indra, the King of heaven, into a calf, and from the earth they milked the beverage soma, which is nectar. Thus they became very powerful in mental speculation and bodily and sensual strength.

verses.sanskrit: दैतेया दानवा वत्सं प्रह्लादमसुरर्षभम् ।विधायादूदुहन् क्षीरमय:पात्रे सुरासवम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: daiteyā dānavā vatsaṁprahlādam asurarṣabhamvidhāyādūduhan kṣīramayaḥ-pātre surāsavam
verses.text: The sons of Diti and the demons transformed Prahlāda Mahārāja, who was born in an asura family, into a calf, and they extracted various kinds of liquor and beer, which they put into a pot made of iron.

verses.sanskrit: गन्धर्वाप्सरसोऽधुक्षन् पात्रे पद्ममये पय: ।वत्सं विश्वावसुं कृत्वा गान्धर्वं मधु सौभगम् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: gandharvāpsaraso ’dhukṣanpātre padmamaye payaḥvatsaṁ viśvāvasuṁ kṛtvāgāndharvaṁ madhu saubhagam
verses.text: The inhabitants of Gandharvaloka and Apsaroloka made Viśvāvasu into a calf, and they drew the milk into a lotus-flower pot. The milk took the shape of sweet musical art and beauty.

verses.sanskrit: वत्सेन पितरोऽर्यम्णा कव्यं क्षीरमधुक्षत ।आमपात्रे महाभागा: श्रद्धया श्राद्धदेवता: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: vatsena pitaro ’ryamṇākavyaṁ kṣīram adhukṣataāma-pātre mahā-bhāgāḥśraddhayā śrāddha-devatāḥ
verses.text: The fortunate inhabitants of Pitṛloka, who preside over the funeral ceremonies, made Aryamā into a calf. With great faith they milked kavya, food offered to the ancestors, into an unbaked earthen pot.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकल्प्य वत्सं कपिलं सिद्धा: सङ्कल्पनामयीम् ।सिद्धिं नभसि विद्यां च ये च विद्याधरादय: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: prakalpya vatsaṁ kapilaṁsiddhāḥ saṅkalpanāmayīmsiddhiṁ nabhasi vidyāṁ caye ca vidyādharādayaḥ
verses.text: After this, the inhabitants of Siddhaloka, as well as the inhabitants of Vidyādhara-loka, transformed the great sage Kapila into a calf, and making the whole sky into a pot, they milked out specific yogic mystic powers, beginning with aṇimā. Indeed, the inhabitants of Vidyādhara-loka acquired the art of flying in the sky.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ये च मायिनो मायामन्तर्धानाद्भुतात्मनाम् ।मयं प्रकल्प्य वत्सं ते दुदुहुर्धारणामयीम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: anye ca māyino māyāmantardhānādbhutātmanāmmayaṁ prakalpya vatsaṁ teduduhur dhāraṇāmayīm
verses.text: Others also, the inhabitants of planets known as Kimpuruṣa-loka, made the demon Maya into a calf, and they milked out mystic powers by which one can disappear immediately from another’s vision and appear again in a different form.

verses.sanskrit: यक्षरक्षांसि भूतानि पिशाचा: पिशिताशना: ।भूतेशवत्सा दुदुहु: कपाले क्षतजासवम् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: yakṣa-rakṣāṁsi bhūtānipiśācāḥ piśitāśanāḥbhūteśa-vatsā duduhuḥkapāle kṣatajāsavam
verses.text: Then the Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, ghosts and witches, who are habituated to eating flesh, transformed Lord Śiva’s incarnation Rudra [Bhūtanātha] into a calf and milked out beverages made of blood and put them in a pot made of skulls.

verses.sanskrit: तथाहयो दन्दशूका: सर्पा नागाश्च तक्षकम् ।विधाय वत्सं दुदुहुर्बिलपात्रे विषं पय: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāhayo dandaśūkāḥsarpā nāgāś ca takṣakamvidhāya vatsaṁ duduhurbila-pātre viṣaṁ payaḥ
verses.text: Thereafter cobras and snakes without hoods, large snakes, scorpions and many other poisonous animals took poison out of the planet earth as their milk and kept this poison in snake holes. They made a calf out of Takṣaka.

verses.sanskrit: पशवो यवसं क्षीरं वत्सं कृत्वा च गोवृषम् ।अरण्यपात्रे चाधुक्षन्मृगेन्द्रेण च दंष्ट्रिण: ॥ २३ ॥क्रव्यादा: प्राणिन: क्रव्यं दुदुहु: स्वे कलेवरे ।सुपर्णवत्सा विहगाश्चरं चाचरमेव च ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: paśavo yavasaṁ kṣīraṁvatsaṁ kṛtvā ca go-vṛṣamaraṇya-pātre cādhukṣanmṛgendreṇa ca daṁṣṭriṇaḥ
verses.text: The four-legged animals like the cows made a calf out of the bull who carries Lord Śiva and made a milking pot out of the forest. Thus they got fresh green grasses to eat. Ferocious animals like tigers transformed a lion into a calf, and thus they were able to get flesh for milk. The birds made a calf out of Garuḍa and took milk from the planet earth in the form of moving insects and nonmoving plants and grasses.

verses.sanskrit: वटवत्सा वनस्पतय: पृथग्रसमयं पय: ।गिरयो हिमवद्वत्सा नानाधातून् स्वसानुषु ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaṭa-vatsā vanaspatayaḥpṛthag rasamayaṁ payaḥgirayo himavad-vatsānānā-dhātūn sva-sānuṣu
verses.text: The trees made a calf out of the banyan tree, and thus they derived milk in the form of many delicious juices. The mountains transformed the Himālayas into a calf, and they milked a variety of minerals into a pot made of the peaks of hills.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे स्वमुख्यवत्सेन स्वे स्वे पात्रे पृथक् पय: ।सर्वकामदुघां पृथ्वीं दुदुहु: पृथुभाविताम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarve sva-mukhya-vatsenasve sve pātre pṛthak payaḥsarva-kāma-dughāṁ pṛthvīṁduduhuḥ pṛthu-bhāvitām
verses.text: The planet earth supplied everyone his respective food. During the time of King Pṛthu, the earth was fully under the control of the King. Thus all the inhabitants of the earth could get their food supply by creating various types of calves and putting their particular types of milk in various pots.

verses.sanskrit: एवं पृथ्वादय: पृथ्वीमन्नादा: स्वन्नमात्मन: ।दोहवत्सादिभेदेन क्षीरभेदं कुरूद्वह ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ pṛthv-ādayaḥ pṛthvīmannādāḥ svannam ātmanaḥdoha-vatsādi-bhedenakṣīra-bhedaṁ kurūdvaha
verses.text: My dear Vidura, chief of the Kurus, in this way King Pṛthu and all the others who subsist on food created different types of calves and milked out their respective eatables. Thus they received their various foodstuffs, which were symbolized as milk.

verses.sanskrit: ततो महीपति: प्रीत: सर्वकामदुघां पृथु: ।दुहितृत्वे चकारेमां प्रेम्णा दुहितृवत्सल: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato mahīpatiḥ prītaḥsarva-kāma-dughāṁ pṛthuḥduhitṛtve cakāremāṁpremṇā duhitṛ-vatsalaḥ
verses.text: Thereafter King Pṛthu was very satisfied with the planet earth, for she sufficiently supplied all food to various living entities. Thus he developed an affection for the planet earth, just as if she were his own daughter.

verses.sanskrit: चूर्णयन् स्वधनुष्कोट्या गिरिकूटानि राजराट् ।भूमण्डलमिदं वैन्य: प्रायश्चक्रे समं विभु: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: cūrṇayan sva-dhanuṣ-koṭyāgiri-kūṭāni rāja-rāṭbhū-maṇḍalam idaṁ vainyaḥprāyaś cakre samaṁ vibhuḥ
verses.text: After this, the king of all kings, Mahārāja Pṛthu, leveled all rough places on the surface of the globe by breaking up the hills with the strength of his bow. By his grace the surface of the globe almost became flat.

verses.sanskrit: अथास्मिन् भगवान् वैन्य: प्रजानां वृत्तिद: पिता ।निवासान् कल्पयां चक्रे तत्र तत्र यथार्हत: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: athāsmin bhagavān vainyaḥprajānāṁ vṛttidaḥ pitānivāsān kalpayāṁ cakretatra tatra yathārhataḥ
verses.text: To all the citizens of the state, King Pṛthu was as good as a father. Thus he was visibly engaged in giving them proper subsistence and proper employment for subsistence. After leveling the surface of the globe, he earmarked different places for residential quarters, inasmuch as they were desirable.

verses.sanskrit: ग्रामान् पुर: पत्तनानि दुर्गाणि विविधानि च ।घोषान् व्रजान् सशिबिरानाकरान् खेटखर्वटान् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: grāmān puraḥ pattanānidurgāṇi vividhāni caghoṣān vrajān sa-śibirānākarān kheṭa-kharvaṭān
verses.text: In this way the King founded many types of villages, settlements and towns and built forts, residences for cowherdsmen, stables for the animals, places for the royal camps, mining places, agricultural towns and mountain villages.

verses.sanskrit: प्राक्पृथोरिह नैवैषा पुरग्रामादिकल्पना ।यथासुखं वसन्ति स्म तत्र तत्राकुतोभया: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāk pṛthor iha naivaiṣāpura-grāmādi-kalpanāyathā-sukhaṁ vasanti smatatra tatrākutobhayāḥ
verses.text: Before the reign of King Pṛthu there was no planned arrangement for different cities, villages, pasturing grounds, etc. Everything was scattered, and everyone constructed his residential quarters according to his own convenience. However, from the time of King Pṛthu, plans were made for towns and villages.
